events.label: reasoning
events.text:
>
```json
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-service",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 100,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "DP impulse lines sloped wrong for service: gas service taps below pipe, liquid service taps on top of pipe",
    "Condensate will collect in gas impulse lines, causing false high differential pressure readings and potential liquid slugging",
    "Gas pockets will form in liquid impulse lines, causing erratic or dampened readings and measurement lag",
    "Incorrect slope prevents self-venting of gas lines and self-draining of liquid lines"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove and re-route impulse lines with correct slope for service: gas lines slope up from transmitter to tap, liquid lines slope down from transmitter to tap",
    "Install condensate pots or seal pots as required by service and manufacturer instructions",
    "Re-zero and calibrate transmitter after re-piping",
    "Inspect adjacent transmitter installations for same defect"
  ],
  "rationale": "The impulse tubing orientation is reversed for the service conditions. In gas service, impulse lines should slope from the transmitter up to the tap so condensate drains back to the process; taps below the pipe trap liquid and cause measurement error. In liquid service, impulse lines should slope down to the transmitter so gas vents back to the process; taps on top trap vapor. This is a classic installation defect that violates good practice in API RP 551 and manufacturer manuals, creating unreliable measurement and potential process upset while the asset is in service.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 551",
    "Manufacturer transmitter installation manuals"
  ]
}
```
